topic: Uniform-in-DGP Neyman orthogonality breakdown frontier for heterogeneous-treatment-effect DML estimators across nuisance classes parametrized by Holder smoothness and metric entropy

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the sharp uniform-in-DGP frontier at which first-order Neyman-orthogonal DML/HTE estimators and their DML-LRO-ADML studentization reports cease to be asymptotically linear and Gaussian as nuisance Holder smoothness, entropy, dimension, and overlap vary.

source_refs.claim: Introduces orthogonal scores plus cross-fitting to remove first-order regularization and overfitting bias under high-dimensional/non-Donsker nuisance estimation, but gives sufficient rate conditions rather than a sharp impossibility frontier.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: Derives minimax rates for Holder-smooth CATE and identifies a nuisance-smoothness elbow where oracle nonparametric-regression rates become unattainable.
source_refs.author: Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, Wasserman

source_refs.claim: Shows finite-sample optimal ATE inference under unconfoundedness depends on smoothness, shape, and overlap restrictions, motivating necessity rather than only sufficient DML conditions.
source_refs.author: Armstrong, Kolesar

source_refs.excerpt: Conjecture 1: spectral studentization-report frontier (NOT YET PROVED)
Replace Assumption reports with explicit DML, LRO, and ADML variance-report summands

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published DML literature supplies orthogonal-score and cross-fitting sufficient conditions, while minimax CATE and finite-sample ATE work expose smoothness and overlap thresholds where oracle or root-n behavior can fail; the prior CausalSmith proposal tried to turn this into an exact report-frontier but reviewers found the DML/LRO/ADML variance summands and scale-free spectral normalization were not yet pinned down.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem, with matching counterexamples, giving the uniform Gaussian-inference and report-equivalence frontier over Holder balls and entropy-indexed nuisance classes, including exact DML/LRO/ADML report-ratio limits above and below the boundary.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize when first-order Neyman orthogonality is insufficient and higher-order orthogonalization is necessary or sufficient for HTE/CATE inference below the nuisance-smoothness elbow.

source_refs.claim: Uses higher-order influence-function ideas to attain minimax CATE rates and shows low nuisance smoothness creates slower mixed rates.
source_refs.author: Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, Wasserman

source_refs.claim: Canonical DML is first-order Neyman orthogonal and requires nuisance learners to be accurate enough that second-order remainders are negligible.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

open_problems.why_unsolved: First-order DML and higher-order/minimax CATE results currently live as separate sufficient theories; the missing obstacle is a sharp impossibility statement showing exactly which smoothness and target-regularity regimes make the first-order orthogonal remainder too large for uniform inference.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A breakdown-frontier theorem indexed by nuisance and target smoothness, with lower bounds proving first-order DML cannot be uniformly valid below the frontier and constructive kth-order orthogonal scores attaining the boundary.

open_problems.open_problem: Primitive CEINR-admissible LRO normalization frontier: characterize exactly which ATE-specialized LRO GMM reports reproduce the DML/ADML Riesz report.

source_refs.excerpt: Conjecture is not yet proved; the open technical step is checking that the displayed ATE-specialized LRO report map
primitive necessary-and-sufficient conditions on CEINR admissible dictionaries, Jacobians, and weights

open_problems.why_unsolved: The rejected prior angle reduced equivalence to a residual-L2-style rho=0 condition, but reviewers found this too tautological unless the dictionaries, Jacobians, and weights are derived from the published LRO first-step influence-function equations and compared to the ADML Riesz normal equations.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient characterization of admissible dictionaries and weights under which optimal-weight LRO aggregation equals the ATE Riesz representer, together with a counterexample dictionary where the equality fails.

open_problems.open_problem: Extend orthogonal statistical learning guarantees from excess-risk oracle rates to uniform confidence bands and simultaneous inference for HTE estimators over entropy-indexed target and nuisance classes.

source_refs.claim: Provides nonasymptotic excess-risk guarantees under Neyman orthogonality and metric entropy conditions, showing nuisance error enters only at second order.
source_refs.author: Foster, Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: Gives DML estimation and simultaneous inference for best linear predictors and group averages of CATE/structural functions using orthogonal signals estimated by modern ML.
source_refs.author: Semenova, Chernozhukov

open_problems.why_unsolved: Excess-risk oracle inequalities do not by themselves yield Gaussian approximation, anti-concentration, or multiplier-bootstrap validity for function-valued HTE learners, especially when target classes are rich enough that entropy and nuisance estimation interact.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniform inference theorem giving entropy and nuisance-rate thresholds for honest confidence bands or simultaneous tests for orthogonal HTE learners, with matching lower bounds when entropy is too large.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the regular-to-nonregular transition for local CATE, subgroup, and shrinking-neighborhood treatment-effect parameters when the Riesz representer norm diverges.

source_refs.claim: Develops debiased ML for global and local linear functionals using regularized Riesz representers, including nonregular local objects.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.claim: Automatic DML learns bias corrections/Riesz representers and gives primitive inference conditions for causal and structural effects.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

open_problems.why_unsolved: Local parameters make the Riesz representer ill-conditioned as bandwidth or subgroup mass shrinks, and current ADML/Riesz results do not draw a sharp uniform boundary between root-n regularity, slower-rate inference, and nonregular limiting behavior.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem bounding the Riesz norm and DML remainder as functions of localization radius, Holder smoothness, overlap, and entropy, with Gaussian or non-Gaussian limit regimes separated explicitly.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a sharp high-dimensional asymptotic-normality frontier for causal forests and generalized random forests with estimated nuisances, rather than fixed low-dimensional honesty and regularity conditions.

source_refs.claim: Develops asymptotically normal causal forests and confidence intervals, while leaving high-dimensional random-forest normality beyond the paper's fixed-regime assumptions.
source_refs.author: Wager, Athey
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Generalized random forests estimate local solutions to orthogonal estimating equations and provide asymptotic theory under honesty and regularity assumptions.
source_refs.author: Athey, Tibshirani, Wager

source_refs.claim: Combines Neyman orthogonality with generalized random forests for HTE estimation and proves oracle-style rates under nuisance-consistency assumptions.
source_refs.author: Oprescu, Syrgkanis, Wu

open_problems.why_unsolved: Forest theory controls adaptivity through honesty and subsampling, while DML controls nuisance bias through orthogonality and cross-fitting; existing results do not give a sharp uniform limit theory when dimension, entropy, overlap, and nuisance complexity all vary together.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem for pointwise and uniform forest-based HTE inference over dimension-growing Holder or sparse nuisance models, including counterexamples where honesty plus orthogonality no longer controls bias.

open_problems.open_problem: Establish sharp nuisance-rate and entropy conditions for automatic DML/Riesz regression when the Riesz representer is learned by flexible black-box learners such as neural nets or forests.

source_refs.claim: Automatic DML estimates bias corrections without requiring the analyst to know the full debiasing formula and can be paired with neural nets, forests, lasso, boosting, and other learners.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.claim: Develops deep neural-network rates for semiparametric estimation and inference with applications to treatment effects and related causal parameters.
source_refs.author: Farrell, Liang, Misra

open_problems.why_unsolved: Automatic debiasing shifts the burden to learning the Riesz representer, whose complexity can differ sharply from the outcome regression; black-box learner risk bounds often do not imply the exact product-rate and stochastic-equicontinuity conditions needed uniformly.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp sufficient-and-necessary learnability condition for the regression and Riesz classes, expressed through entropy, smoothness, and approximation rates, with worked examples for forests and ReLU networks.

literature_map:
Chernozhukov et al. (2018) is the DML anchor: orthogonal scores plus cross-fitting neutralize first-order nuisance bias under sufficient product-rate and entropy conditions.
Belloni, Chernozhukov, and Hansen (2014) and Belloni et al. (2017) give uniform post-selection and program-evaluation inference under approximate sparsity, a different complexity regime from Holder smoothness.
Semenova and Chernozhukov (2021) move DML toward CATE and other structural functions through orthogonal signals, best-linear-predictor targets, group averages, and simultaneous inference.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrica) automate debiasing through learned Riesz representers, broadening DML beyond hand-built scores.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrics Journal) emphasize global versus local linear functionals, where local targets become nonregular as the Riesz norm grows.
Foster and Syrgkanis (2023) provide orthogonal statistical learning with metric-entropy and local-complexity excess-risk bounds, but not a full uniform-inference frontier.
Wager and Athey (2018), Athey, Tibshirani, and Wager (2019), and Oprescu, Syrgkanis, and Wu (2019) supply forest-based HTE inference through honesty, local estimating equations, and orthogonality.
Farrell, Liang, and Misra (2021) connect deep neural-network rates to semiparametric causal inference, raising the question of sharp DML conditions for modern overparameterized learners.
Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman (2024) give minimax CATE theory with a nuisance-smoothness elbow and higher-order influence-function construction.
Armstrong and Kolesar (2021) show finite-sample honest ATE inference can require larger critical values and minimum smoothness/overlap conditions, reinforcing the need for necessity frontiers.
The main tension is that DML papers often state sufficient nuisance-rate conditions, while minimax and finite-sample papers expose thresholds where orthogonality alone cannot buy root-n inference.
The live open substrate is the exact boundary, over Holder smoothness and metric entropy, where first-order orthogonal HTE DML changes from uniformly valid to biased, slower-rate, or nonregular.
prior_proposal_map:
Target qid eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier/v1 currently has only init pipeline/state files, with no proposal or reviews to mine.
Most relevant signals came from failed bank entry eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2, a close DML/LRO/ADML orthogonality and studentization-frontier predecessor.
The active proposal exposed Conjecture 1 on rho_star and Conjecture 2 on LRO normalization equivalence as NOT YET PROVED kernels.
Rejected angle1 pivoted from weak-overlap rate frontiers to CEINR-admissible LRO normalization classification via residual-variance L2 quotients.
Reviewer JSONs repeatedly flagged missing exact report summands, scale-invariant spectral normalization, primitive LRO admissibility, and finite-support witnesses.
Bank README confirms v2 failed at Stage -0.5 after all angles were exhausted, not because the frontier was solved.
No repository evidence found for Holder-smoothness or metric-entropy nuisance-class frontiers; the closest available open problems are nuisance-rate, weak-overlap, Riesz, and report-normalization frontiers.
